changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "The Slacking Manager"

TextENGNew: ``Shion-san, why don't you help out a little?``
"Are you asking a girl to carry something heavy? This is why you're still single at your age, Coach."
``Well, you see, at times like these, here's what people say. Ahem—it's none of your business.``
``Ahahahahahahaha.``
We were loading up the station wagon with all the equipment from today's barbecue.
changeReason: Old translation says 'He was loading' (singular), but the new says 'We were loading' (collective), better matching the context of cleanup where everyone participates.

TextENGNew: The parents who had helped were already starting to head home.
changeReason: Old translation says 'fathers and sons' but the Japanese 父兄 means 'parents/guardians,' not literally fathers and sons.

TextENGNew: ``...All right. So, Shion-san...@k@vS20/10/441000027. Why did you come all the way here?@k@vS20/10/441000028. If you rode here on your bike, I can give you a lift.``
``I did, but I'll be fine riding back. I appreciate your consideration.``
"......I was glad you came today. Things just aren't the same without our manager around."
"Am I still the manager? I've been shirking for an entire year now. You should really fire such a useless manager already."
``All right. If you say you want to quit, then I'll fire you whenever you want.``
``...Tch. That's not fair.
.........Let me go soon, please? Every time I don't show up at a game, I get an annoying phone call, and I can't stand those.``
``If you were more honest with yourself, Shion-chan, you'd be far cuter. Your father is so sad—where did he go so wrong as to deserve such a rebellious daughter?
Oh, perhaps if your father catches you in a big, loving embrace, you'll open your eyes?! Well then, don't mind if I do!!
Come here~~~♪♪♪``
...Coach was doing his best to clown around, but Shion only gazed into the distance with a faint smile, not taking the bait.
"......Nothing has changed. You're still the same weirdo, Coach, and everyone's still as lively as ever."
"...Yet Satoshi-kun alone is absent. ...Is that what you want to say?"
``He'll come home. I'm sure of it.@k@vS20/10/441000036. He has someone waiting for him.``
``......Hmph. ...Say whatever you want.``
"He really is a heartless one, isn't he? Running off to who knows where and leaving such a lovely girlfriend behind."
``Pfft... hu hu hu, ha ha ha ha ha ha!``
``...Pff... geez! Quit laughing already!!@k@vS20/06/440600026. Coooach!!``
``Ha ha ha! Wahh ha ha ha!!``
For a good while, Coach doubled over, laughing until his sides hurt...
``We'll see each other again. Please come and root for us once in a while, okay?``
``If I feel like it. ...I have no desire to be the manager anymore, after all.``
``...That's fine. If you don't like it, I'll fire you.@k@vS20/10/441000042. I'm not the kind of person who likes forcing people to do things against their will.``

"..................Tch. Fine, fine!@k@vS20/06/440600029.I'm sorry, I'm sorry!@k@vS20/06/440600030.You win.@k@vS20/06/440600031.I'll come cheer you on when I feel like it, so just let it go for today."
changeReason: Old translation says 'just be patient,' but the Japanese 堪忍して下さい means 'please forgive me/let it go,' a request for pardon rather than patience.

TextENGNew: ``Hu hu hu! Whoops, that time already?@k@vS20/10/441000044. It's past time to return all this...!@k@vS20/10/441000045. I'm already making them wait for me!@k@vS20/10/441000046. I'll be leaving for today, then!@k@vS20/10/441000047. Let's meet again at the next game!``
``Yes, if I feel like it. Good-bye, Coach.``
